title: quercus × calvescens subsp. costae
intro: Quercus × calvescens subsp. costae, also known as the hybrid white oak, is a rare and hybrid species of oak tree that combines characteristics of the Coast live oak (Quercus agrifolia) and the valley oak (Quercus lobata). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Quercus × calvescens subsp. costae include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in tinctures, teas, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to digestive issues.
